perturbation.original_text: “ ​ Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means all information, whether visual, written, electronic or oral, (including any information furnished prior to the execution of this Agreement) related to the products, services, technology, business, customers, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) or prospects of a Disclosing Party which is furnished by such Disclosing Party, or otherwise disclosed by such Disclosing Party, to a Receiving Party in connection with the consideration and evaluation of the Transaction (including, without limitation, samples, compounds and ingredients thereof, prototypes, formulae, technical know how, technical data, financial information, payment terms, performance data, product specifications and customer and/or supplier information) and all reports, analyses, compilations, data, studies and other materials (in whatever form maintained, whether documentary, computer storage or otherwise) UNITED STATES ​ | 545 W. Lake St. | Elmhurst, IL 60126 | 800.831.7294 MEXICO ​ | Nicolas Gogol #11332-A | Complejo Industrial Chihuahua | Chihuahua, Chih. 31136 www.chiefent.com Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 1 of 6 Rev. 25-Feb-2020 prepared by the Receiving Party containing or reflecting such furnished information. All such Information shall be identified as confidential and/or proprietary in accordance with the following: (i) in the case of written or electronic information, such Information shall be identified at the time of disclosure with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification on the face thereof or by an accompanying written statement; (ii) in the case of visual information, such Information shall be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or as soon thereafter as is reasonably practicable; and (iii) with respect to oral information, such information shall be reduced to written or language form, marked as confidential with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification, and transmitted to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days following the oral disclosure. The term “ ​ Confidential Information ​ ” does not include information (i) which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits any Information, (ii) which is or becomes known or available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis and not in contravention of applicable law from a source (other than the Disclosing Party) which has represented to the Receiving Party (which the Receiving Party has no reason to disbelieve after due inquiry) that it is entitled to disclose it to the Receiving Party on such basis or (iii) which a Receiving Party can prove (1) was lawfully in its possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party or (2) was developed by the Receiving Party independently of any Information received from the Disclosing Party and without otherwise violating any of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: “ ​ Confidential ​ Information ​ ” means all information, whether visual, written, electronic or oral, (including any information furnished prior to the execution of this Agreement) related to the products, services, technology, business, customers, working capital, affairs, assets, condition (financial or otherwise) or prospects of a Disclosing Party which is furnished by such Disclosing Party, or otherwise disclosed by such Disclosing Party, to a Receiving Party in connection with the consideration and evaluation of the Transaction (including, without limitation, samples, compounds and ingredients thereof, prototypes, formulae, technical know how, technical data, financial information, payment terms, performance data, product specifications and customer and/or supplier information) and all reports, analyses, compilations, data, studies and other materials (in whatever form maintained, whether documentary, computer storage or otherwise) UNITED STATES ​ | 545 W. Lake St. | Elmhurst, IL 60126 | 800.831.7294 MEXICO ​ | Nicolas Gogol #11332-A | Complejo Industrial Chihuahua | Chihuahua, Chih. 31136 www.chiefent.com Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 1 of 6 Rev. 25-Feb-2020 prepared by the Receiving Party containing or reflecting such furnished information. All such Information shall only be considered confidential and/or proprietary if it is conspicuously identified as such at the time of disclosure by an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party, and that designation is acknowledged in writing by the Receiving Party within five business days of the disclosure. All such Information shall be identified as confidential and/or proprietary in accordance with the following: (i) in the case of written or electronic information, such Information shall be identified at the time of disclosure with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification on the face thereof or by an accompanying written statement; (ii) in the case of visual information, such Information shall be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or as soon thereafter as is reasonably practicable; and (iii) with respect to oral information, such information shall be reduced to written or language form, marked as confidential with an appropriate legend, marking, stamp, or positive written identification, and transmitted to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days following the oral disclosure. The term “ ​ Confidential Information ​ ” does not include information (i) which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits any Information, (ii) which is or becomes known or available to the Receiving Party on a non-confidential basis and not in contravention of applicable law from a source (other than the Disclosing Party) which has represented to the Receiving Party (which the Receiving Party has no reason to disbelieve after due inquiry) that it is entitled to disclose it to the Receiving Party on such basis or (iii) which a Receiving Party can prove (1) was lawfully in its possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party or (2) was developed by the Receiving Party independently of any Information received from the Disclosing Party and without otherwise violating any of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a requirement for the Receiving Party to acknowledge the confidentiality designation in writing within five business days, which creates ambiguity. The original text states that all information related to the transaction should be treated as confidential as long as it's identified as such at the time of disclosure, with specific guidelines for written, visual, and oral information. The additional requirement creates a contradiction because information may initially be considered confidential, but if the Receiving Party fails to acknowledge it within five days, its status becomes unclear, impacting the enforcement of confidentiality, especially in section 2, where use of confidential information is discussed. Specifically, the requirement for written acknowledgement within 5 days contradicts with the statement about samples, compounds and ingredients, prototypes, formula etc, for the whole evaluation of the transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a new requirement for written acknowledgement of confidentiality within five business days, which directly contradicts the original definition of 'Confidential Information' and its established procedures for identifying confidential material (written, visual, oral). This new condition creates uncertainty regarding the status of confidential information if acknowledgement is delayed, thus conflicting with the contradicted text that requires the Receiving Party to keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and may use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, only for internal evaluation purposes related to the Transaction, but not for any external communications or business development efforts. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall be construed in a way to grant to the Receiving Party a limited right or license with respect to the Information to use such Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an internal contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party 'may use' the Confidential Information but only for 'internal evaluation purposes', but the original definition in section 1(b) states Confidential Information can be used in evaluation of the transaction. This creates confusion around what constitutes acceptable use, since the original language is less restrictive. The term may use contradictss with the original definition of confidential information. This contradictory language affects the interpretation of section 5, which defines the scope of rights, creating ambiguity around the permissible use of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No license, intellectual property right or other ownership or use right is conveyed solely by this Agreement, except the limited right to use Confidential Information described in Sections 1 and 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the scope of permissible use of Confidential Information, stating it 'may use' the information only for 'internal evaluation purposes'. This contradicts the original text which allowed usage 'in connection with evaluating the Transaction' without explicitly limiting it to internal purposes. The contradicted text states the limited right to use confidential information described in Sections 1 and 2 of this Agreement. By narrowing the permissible use, the changed text introduces ambiguity and a direct conflict with the broad permission granted in the original text, leading to an in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly following the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall, as directed by the Disclosing Party, (i) destroy (and confirm such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party) or (ii) return all Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party and all copies thereof, as well as all other materials containing Confidential Information in the Receiving Party’s possession (including, without limitation, notes), without retaining any copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly following the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall, as directed by the Disclosing Party, (i) destroy (and confirm such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party) or (ii) return all Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party and all copies thereof, as well as all other materials containing Confidential Information in the Receiving Party’s possession (including, without limitation, notes), without retaining any copies or extracts thereof, unless otherwise required by law or internal compliance policies.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception ('unless otherwise required by law or internal compliance policies') to the requirement for the Receiving Party to return or destroy all Confidential Information upon request. This contradicts the general confidentiality obligations outlined earlier in the agreement, particularly in Section 2(a), where the Receiving Party is required to keep all information confidential. If internal compliance policies allow retention in some cases, it creates uncertainty about whether the Receiving Party is obligated to destroy all copies, leading to ambiguity in enforcement of confidentiality. This creates an ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, and shall not use such Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. The exposure of the Receiving Party to the Confidential Information or its disclosure shall not be construed in any way to grant to the Receiving Party any right or license with respect to the Information other than the right to use such Confidential Information strictly in accordance with this Agreement. Moreover, each Receiving Party shall have the right Confidentiality Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 Page 2 of 6 Rev. 08-Oct-18 to disclose that it is evaluating the Transaction and transmit Confidential Information to its Representatives only if and to the extent that such Representatives need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and agree (in writing) to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any actions by its Affiliates and/or Representatives which are not in accordance with the agreements herein regarding the conduct of its Affiliates and/or Representatives. Visitors shall not take photographs or otherwise record the activities at the other party’s facilities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the requirement for the Receiving Party to return or destroy all Confidential Information upon request, introducing 'unless otherwise required by law or internal compliance policies'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that 'Each Receiving Party shall keep all Confidential Information it receives or obtains from a Disclosing Party confidential'. The exception in the changed text directly clashes with the absolute confidentiality requirement stated in the contradicted text, making the terms inconsistent and creating ambiguity about the obligation to destroy all copies. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of inducing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to cease doing business or reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to purchase, lease, or utilize products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of potentially influencing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to consider alternative business arrangements or potentially reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to explore products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises.
perturbation.explanation: The original non-solicitation clause prohibits the Company from soliciting clients to 'cease doing business or reduce their business' with Chief Enterprises. The modified version uses the phrase 'potentially influencing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to consider alternative business arrangements or potentially reduce their business'. Replacing the explicit prohibitions with softer, more ambiguous language creates a contradiction. In the first section the word solicitation has been defined as something stronger than influencing. Now introducing potentially influencing contradicts with the word solictation. This now contradicts and makes the original text not clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for two (2) years following its termination, Company will not directly or indirectly, solicit or assist others in soliciting, or attempt to solicit or assist others in attempting to solicit, (i) any existing customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information, or (ii) any prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors of Chief Enterprises with or about whom the Company obtained Confidential Information and with whom Chief Enterprises took significant steps to do business with during the term of this Agreement, for the purpose or effect of potentially influencing such existing or prospective customers, clients, suppliers, business partners, and/or vendors to consider alternative business arrangements or potentially reduce their business with Chief Enterprises or to explore products or services that are competitive with, similar to, or that may be used as substitutes for any products or services offered by Chief Enterprises.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text are actually the same in this case, which can happen and doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction. The change from a prohibition on actions that 'cease doing business or reduce their business' to actions that 'potentially influence such existing or prospective customers...to consider alternative business arrangements or potentially reduce their business' weakens the restriction. This introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of prohibited solicitation. This ambiguity contradicts the original intent of the non-solicitation clause by creating uncertainty about which behaviors are restricted. The change makes enforcement unclear and the original text more open to interpretation. The explanation indicates that the word solicitation has been defined as something stronger than influencing, and introducing 'potentially influencing' contradicts with the word solictation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be amended, altered or modified except by an instrument in writing expressly referring to this Agreement and signed by the Parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended, altered or modified by mutual agreement of the parties. Such agreement should be documented in writing where feasible, but verbal agreements altering operational procedures under this agreement will be considered binding so long as both parties demonstrate clear acceptance of such changes in practice.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement can be amended, altered, or modified not only by a written instrument, but also by verbal agreements as long as there is clear acceptance of such changes in practice. This directly contradicts the original clause, which explicitly requires any amendment to be in writing and signed by both parties, creating ambiguity around the validity of any alteration made without a written agreement. The introduction of possibility of a verbal alteration in a contract that mentions that the agreement should not be amended unless in writing signed by both parties is an In-Text contradiction. This contradiction places doubt to what is previously stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be amended, altered or modified by mutual agreement of the parties. Such agreement should be documented in writing where feasible, but verbal agreements altering operational procedures under this agreement will be considered binding so long as both parties demonstrate clear acceptance of such changes in practice.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that any amendment, alteration, or modification must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text introduces the possibility of verbal agreements altering operational procedures, as long as both parties demonstrate clear acceptance in practice. This directly contradicts the original clause, creating ambiguity about the validity of alterations made without a written agreement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, further solidifying the contradiction introduced by the modification. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for so long as such Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, information or property which is protected by law for a longer period of time (e.g. trade secrets) shall continue to receive all protections to which it is entitled for so long as it qualifies for protection under the applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for five (5) years, irrespective of whether such Confidential Information becomes public. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, information or property which is protected by law for a longer period of time (e.g. trade secrets) shall continue to receive all protections to which it is entitled for so long as it qualifies for protection under the applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the duration of confidentiality obligations for Confidential Information. Originally, the obligations last 'for so long as such Confidential Information remains confidential and non-public.' The changed text states that the confidentiality obligations last for five (5) years, 'irrespective of whether such Confidential Information becomes public.' This creates a contradiction because the original clause implied that if the information becomes public, the obligations cease, but the revised clause imposes a fixed term regardless of the information's public status. The change in the time frame for confidentiality is an in-text contradiction, due to the condition not being the duration of the information being confidential, now the duration will be fixed. The five years now contradicts with what was mentioned with confidential remains confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, provided that (i) either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving 60 days’ written notice in accordance with the notice provisions hereof and (ii) with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder, the confidentiality obligations set forth in Paragraph 2(a) shall remain in full force and effect for five (5) years, irrespective of whether such Confidential Information becomes public. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, information or property which is protected by law for a longer period of time (e.g. trade secrets) shall continue to receive all protections to which it is entitled for so long as it qualifies for protection under the applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that confidentiality obligations ended when the information became public. The changed text imposes a fixed five-year confidentiality period, regardless of whether the information becomes public. This creates a direct contradiction because the original condition, confidentiality, is no longer relevant to the duration, as it is now a fixed duration. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, but the important element to consider is that this text contradicts with the original text's terms of confidentiality duration.
